behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output content (signature fidelity), so a stub would hide real failures (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text only requires one generation decision, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real model generation from the natural-language spec, parse the produced function signature, and assert exact match on parameter names/types and return type; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/state flow in orchestrator (attempt sequencing + early stop), not model quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, no dependence on real AI output, no multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic fakes: attempt1 fails, attempt2 passes, attempt3 would fail if called. Assert calls occur in order and third attempt is never executed.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Chosen test exercises cross-module ordering/early-return, which is above Unit but below System/Agentic.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps (generate -> test feedback -> refine -> retest) and success depends on non-deterministic model behavior across attempts (LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP). Rejected Agentic because single-step agentic tests cannot validate iterative recovery dynamics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real model and real iterative loop on a benchmark prompt known to often fail first pass; verify within <=3 attempts one later attempt passes the suite and loop stops on first pass.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: This is data handoff from test runner/bookkeeping into prompt-construction module. Rejected Agentic because behavior text checks prompt content inclusion, not model judgment quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no real AI output dependency for assertion; no multi-agentic chain needed.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one failed attempt with stubbed model output and deterministic failing test results; capture the next prompt payload and assert it contains exact failing test names and failure messages.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could be unit if only string formatting, but behavior explicitly targets cross-module handoff into next prompt.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs at least two real agentic decisions in sequence and validates that prior-step failure context influences next-step generation (orchestration + model behavior across steps). Rejected Integration because deterministic stubs can force different code and miss whether the real model actually responds to context.
behaviors.test_description: Run live loop with real model through at least two attempts after an induced first failure; compare generated code artifacts and assert attempt N+1 is meaningfully changed and conditioned on failure context, not identical resend.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestrator state/bookkeeping across modules (attempt counter, aggregation, final error shape). Rejected System because behavior text does not require real subprocess/network/OS faults; rejected Agentic because deterministic failures are sufficient and assertion is infrastructure/state, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic always-failing generation/test fakes, run orchestrator, assert exactly 3 attempts executed, loop terminates, and returned error includes all three attempt records with per-attempt test results.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is needed for state propagation across components; Unit would miss wiring defects.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says non-agentic and deterministic stubs; target is multi-module wiring end-to-end. Rejected System because wording does not require real OS/network/subprocess behavior; rejected Unit because it is full pipeline wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline in-process with deterministic stub model outputs and deterministic test executor responses; assert generate->execute->bookkeep transitions and final status are correct.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Side-check against Integration->System: no explicit real infra requirement in behavior text.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly requires full multi-step live agent loop and success after refinement, so correctness depends on sequential model decisions (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP). Rejected Agentic because single-call agentic tests cannot validate full loop recovery behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real end-to-end live loop on a curated hard task that is known to fail first attempt; verify first attempt fails, a later attempt passes within limit, and final returned implementation passes suite.
